aggregation_rule: 5-judge primary panel: per-judge per-question -> per-judge per-subject mean -> panel mean across {haiku, sonnet, opus, gpt4o, gpt54}; 7-judge sensitivity adds {gemini_flash, gemini_pro}

3_7_4_spearman_5judge_min.estimand: Minimum pairwise Spearman rho across 5-judge primary panel pairs
3_7_4_spearman_5judge_min.aggregation_rule: Spearman rho on per-(subject, condition) judge means (scipy.stats.spearmanr)
3_7_4_spearman_5judge_min.source: 14 main-study subjects' per-(subject, condition, judge, question) scores

3_7_4_spearman_5judge_max.estimand: Maximum pairwise Spearman rho across 5-judge primary panel pairs
3_7_4_spearman_5judge_max.aggregation_rule: Spearman rho on per-(subject, condition) judge means
3_7_4_spearman_5judge_max.source: 14 main-study subjects' per-(subject, condition, judge, question) scores

3_7_4_spearman_7judge_min.estimand: Minimum pairwise Spearman rho across 7-judge sensitivity panel pairs
3_7_4_spearman_7judge_min.aggregation_rule: Spearman rho on per-(subject, condition) judge means
3_7_4_spearman_7judge_min.source: 14 main-study subjects' per-(subject, condition, judge, question) scores

3_7_4_spearman_7judge_max.estimand: Maximum pairwise Spearman rho across 7-judge sensitivity panel pairs
3_7_4_spearman_7judge_max.aggregation_rule: Spearman rho on per-(subject, condition) judge means
3_7_4_spearman_7judge_max.source: 14 main-study subjects' per-(subject, condition, judge, question) scores

3_7_4_krippendorff_alpha_5judge.estimand: Krippendorff's alpha (interval scale) across 5-judge primary panel
3_7_4_krippendorff_alpha_5judge.aggregation_rule: pair-count formula on (subject, condition, question_id) units; pooled across 5 main conditions
3_7_4_krippendorff_alpha_5judge.source: 14 main-study subjects, all per-question per-judge scores
3_7_4_krippendorff_alpha_5judge.note: Recompute uses interval-scale Krippendorff alpha; paper cites 0.659 as ordinal. Both report directional agreement on absolute magnitude. The two scales agree to within ~0.01 on this score distribution but are not the same statistic.

3_7_4_krippendorff_alpha_7judge.estimand: Krippendorff's alpha (interval scale) across 7-judge sensitivity panel
3_7_4_krippendorff_alpha_7judge.aggregation_rule: pair-count formula on (subject, condition, question_id) units; pooled across 5 main conditions
3_7_4_krippendorff_alpha_7judge.source: 14 main-study subjects, all per-question per-judge scores
3_7_4_krippendorff_alpha_7judge.note: Paper cites ordinal 0.535. Interval-vs-ordinal note same as 5-judge alpha.

3_7_2_strictness_haiku.estimand: Mean per-judge score on abstention-pattern responses (low-baseline slice)
3_7_2_strictness_haiku.aggregation_rule: mean of per-(qid, condition, judge) score across all abstention rows

3_7_2_strictness_sonnet.estimand: Mean per-judge score on abstention-pattern responses (low-baseline slice)
3_7_2_strictness_sonnet.aggregation_rule: mean of per-(qid, condition, judge) score across all abstention rows

3_7_2_strictness_opus.estimand: Mean per-judge score on abstention-pattern responses (low-baseline slice)
3_7_2_strictness_opus.aggregation_rule: mean of per-(qid, condition, judge) score across all abstention rows

3_7_2_strictness_gpt4o.estimand: Mean per-judge score on abstention-pattern responses (low-baseline slice)
3_7_2_strictness_gpt4o.aggregation_rule: mean of per-(qid, condition, judge) score across all abstention rows

3_7_2_strictness_gpt54.estimand: Mean per-judge score on abstention-pattern responses (low-baseline slice)
3_7_2_strictness_gpt54.aggregation_rule: mean of per-(qid, condition, judge) score across all abstention rows

3_7_6_length_corr_overall.estimand: Pearson r between response length (chars) and 5-judge primary score

3_7_6_length_corr_C5.estimand: Pearson r between response length and 5-judge primary score (condition=C5_baseline)

3_7_6_length_corr_C2a.estimand: Pearson r between response length and 5-judge primary score (condition=C2a_full_spec)

3_7_6_length_corr_C4.estimand: Pearson r between response length and 5-judge primary score (condition=C4_factdump)

3_7_6_length_corr_C4a.estimand: Pearson r between response length and 5-judge primary score (condition=C4a_full_facts_plus_spec)

3_7_6_abstention_n_total.estimand: Number of abstention-pattern responses in low-baseline slice

3_7_6_abstention_pct_below_2.estimand: Percent of abstention rows scoring in the 1.0-1.5 band on 5-judge primary
3_7_6_abstention_pct_below_2.note: Paper §3.7.6 quotes 82.8% in the '1.0-1.5 band' which corresponds to score < 1.5, not score < 2.0.

3_7_6_abstention_mean_score.estimand: Mean 5-judge primary score on abstention-pattern responses

3_7_6_high_score_chars_avg.estimand: Mean response length (chars) for ultra-high-scoring responses (>=4.5)

3_7_6_mid_score_chars_avg.estimand: Mean response length (chars) for mid-range-scoring responses (2.5-3.5)

3_4_battery_leakage_n_leaks.estimand: Behavioral-prediction questions with verbatim 7-gram overlap to held-out passages

3_4_battery_leakage_pct_aggregate.estimand: Percent of all BP questions that leak (15 subjects, including Franklin)

3_4_battery_leakage_pct_main_study.estimand: Percent of BP questions that leak in 14 main-study subjects (Franklin excluded)

3_2_franklin_C5_5judge.estimand: Franklin C5 (no-context baseline) on 5-judge primary panel
3_2_franklin_C5_5judge.aggregation_rule: 5-judge primary panel mean (per-judge per-question -> per-judge mean -> panel mean)

3_2_franklin_C5_haiku_only.estimand: Franklin C5 baseline as scored by Haiku alone
3_2_franklin_C5_haiku_only.aggregation_rule: mean of haiku_score across all C5 questions

3_2_franklin_C2a_5judge.estimand: Franklin C2a (spec only) on 5-judge primary panel

3_2_franklin_C4a_5judge.estimand: Franklin C4a (facts + spec) on 5-judge primary panel

3_2_franklin_C5_7judge_min.estimand: Min per-judge mean on Franklin C5 across all available judges (sensitivity)
3_2_franklin_C5_7judge_min.aggregation_rule: min across {haiku, sonnet, opus, gpt4o, gpt54, gemini} per-judge C5 means
3_2_franklin_C5_7judge_min.note: Legacy Franklin run pre-dates the gemini_flash/gemini_pro split; only one Gemini judge is present in legacy data. Range is min/max of per-judge means rather than 5-judge / 7-judge panel means because Franklin is a high-baseline reference, not a main-study subject.

3_2_franklin_C5_7judge_max.estimand: Max per-judge mean on Franklin C5 across all available judges (sensitivity)
